data.body: Plan-review PASS (advisory=20, signed). Design refined per the (correct) advisories: (1) deep-link is a SEPARATE structured 'guide_url' field on each coaching note (NOT appended to the locked-template prose), added in a PLAN-REVIEW-specific enrichment step — NOT in the shared review_kernel.render_coach_notes (passes.render_coach_notes is a re-export used by code-review too). Additive => passthrough-safe for the 5+ coaching consumers (plan_review/code_review workflow_ops, orchestrator, sidecars, gate_dispatch). (2) 'rebar explain' = an EARLY intercept in _cli/__init__.py main() (like review-plan/criteria) + a help/.txt file — NOT known_subcommands (that is .txt-package-data-driven, checked AFTER intercepts). (3) the explain MCP tool goes in _mcp_reads.py register_read_tools (a pure registry/file READ, no LLM call — must NOT be gated behind REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM). (4) anchor rule canonicalized to '#<criterion-id lower-cased>' matching the guide's per-heading slug; a guide-anchor parity check guards renames. (5) explain_criterion is the ONE shared lookup (registry.py) reading the generated guide; errors: unknown-id / malformed-registry / missing-file. (6) REBAR_DOCS_URL: a NARROW env-reading helper in config.py (AC4 says config.py) — env else repo-relative file:// to the guide; NOT a typed TOML key (avoids config-surface proliferation). Guide is GENERATED from the registry + a parity check extends validate-routing.
